title: kalosanthes versicolor
side_effects: Kalosanthes versicolor can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing liver damage due to its high concentration of toxic compounds, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea and vomiting after ingesting it. Additionally, excessive consumption of the plant can lead to seizures and coma in some individuals. If used topically, Kalosanthes versicolor can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and burns, especially on sensitive skin. In rare cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to kidney failure and respiratory problems.
